title: Engine API: start_workflow, cancel, result, list_workflows, shutdown
purpose: Expose the runtime embedding API consumers actually call. Engine wraps the lifecycle transitions, the registry, the store, and the runtime into start_workflow, cancel, result, list_workflows, and a graceful shutdown.
task: Implement engine/engine.rs: the Engine type returned by build(), exposing start_workflow (delegating to the start transition and returning a WorkflowHandle), cancel (delegating to the cancel transition), result (awaiting completion/failure and returning the Payload or WorkflowError), list_workflows (live handles from the registry plus terminal workflows from the store query), and shutdown (graceful drain). Scope is these methods; signal/query/subscribe are AE-013.

requirements.title: start_workflow and cancel
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose start_workflow(workflow_type, input: Payload) -> Result<WorkflowHandle, EngineError> delegating to the start transition, and cancel(&id, &run, reason) -> Result<(), EngineError> delegating to the cancel transition.
acceptance: engine.rs defines Engine holding the RuntimeHandle, the Arc<dyn EventStore>, the registry, the supervision tree, and the loaded-workflow records.
start_workflow calls the AE-008 start operation and returns the WorkflowHandle.
cancel calls the AE-009 cancel transition and returns Ok or EngineError::WorkflowNotFound.
A test starts then cancels a workflow against InMemoryStore and asserts the resulting history is WorkflowStarted followed by WorkflowCancelled.

requirements.title: result()
requirements.spec: WHEN result(&id, &run) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL await the workflow's terminal transition and return the result Payload on completion or the WorkflowError on failure. IF the workflow id/run is unknown and has no terminal history, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::WorkflowNotFound.
acceptance: result awaits the completion-notification channel resolved by the AE-009 complete/fail transitions and returns Result<Payload, WorkflowError> wrapped in EngineError where appropriate.
For an already-terminal workflow, result reads the terminal event from the store and returns its Payload/WorkflowError without blocking forever.
An unknown id/run with no history returns EngineError::WorkflowNotFound.
A test starts a workflow that completes and asserts result returns the recorded Payload; a test for a failing workflow asserts the WorkflowError.

requirements.title: list_workflows
requirements.spec: WHEN list_workflows(filter: WorkflowFilter) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return WorkflowSummary values for live workflows from the registry and for terminal workflows from the store query, deduplicated and matching the filter.
acceptance: list_workflows builds summaries from live registry handles and merges them with the store's query(filter) results, with no duplicate entry for a workflow present in both.
The returned summaries match the WorkflowFilter (type/status/time-range/parent) semantics from aion-core.
A test with one running and one completed workflow asserts both appear once, with correct statuses, and that a status filter narrows the result.

requirements.title: Graceful shutdown
requirements.spec: WHEN shutdown() is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL stop accepting new starts, allow in-flight store appends to finish, drain and stop the runtime scheduler, and return once beamr has stopped. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT drop the runtime to stop it.
acceptance: shutdown sets a flag so subsequent start_workflow calls return an EngineError (shutting-down) rather than spawning.
shutdown waits for outstanding store appends initiated by lifecycle transitions to complete before stopping the runtime.
shutdown calls RuntimeHandle::shutdown to drain and stop the scheduler and returns Ok once beamr has stopped.
A workflow mid-execution at shutdown is left with its durable history intact (Suspended), not force-completed.
A test builds an Engine, starts and completes a workflow, calls shutdown, and asserts it returns Ok and a subsequent start_workflow is rejected.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement signal/query/subscribe — those are AE-013.
SHALL NOT implement HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — Engine is transport-agnostic (CO6); networking is AW.
SHALL NOT reimplement lifecycle transitions — Engine delegates to AE-008/AE-009.
SHALL NOT force-complete or discard in-flight workflows on shutdown — they remain replayable via their durable history (D11).
verification: cargo test -p aion passes against InMemoryStore.
shutdown drains rather than drops (D11); start after shutdown is rejected.
